meta.title: Add synced note pinning
meta.summary: Pins are persisted per-workspace in a versioned .hubble/config.json, loaded on workspace open, kept in sync on pin/unpin/rename/delete, and surfaced as a top 'Pinned' section in the file sidebar. Toggle between the four views to orient yourself, then follow each guided tour.

graphs.label: System overview
graphs.summary: The desktop file-sidebar subsystem: a reactive store, an Electron IPC bridge, and a shared presentational tree renderer.

nodes.title: Workspace store + persistence
nodes.summary: The desktop app keeps workspace state (open folder, file list, sort mode, recent workspaces) in a reactive @simplestack/store. UI subscribes via useStoreValue and re-renders on change. A persistence layer hydrates from and serializes a subset to localStorage so the chosen workspace and preferences survive restarts.
details: workspaceStore is the single source of truth read by the sidebar.
persistence.ts decides which fields are durable vs. recomputed on open.

nodes.title: Store actions layer
nodes.summary: actions.ts is the controller between user intent and the rest of the system. Functions like openWorkspace, refreshFiles, renameMarkdownFile and deleteMarkdownFile mutate the store with workspaceStore.set and call the desktop API for filesystem work. Async errors surface as toasts rather than throwing into the UI.
details: Actions own ordering: state mutation, disk I/O, then a refresh.
Guards like isInWorkspace keep operations scoped to the open folder.

nodes.title: Desktop API IPC bridge
nodes.summary: The renderer never touches the filesystem directly. desktopApi (defined in preload.ts) forwards typed calls over Electron IPC to ipcMain.handle handlers in main.ts. A grants system (assertGrantedRoot / grantFile) restricts reads and writes to user-approved workspace roots, so the renderer can only reach files the user opened.
details: preload.ts is the contract; main.ts is the privileged implementation.
Path validation in main.ts blocks absolute paths and '..' traversal.

nodes.title: Shared UI Sidebar
nodes.summary: packages/ui Sidebar is a platform-agnostic presentational component. It receives a flat files array plus on* callbacks (select, rename, delete, reveal) and renders rows, inline editing, and a per-row actions menu. It holds no app state, so the same component can be driven by different hosts.
details: The desktop app adapts store state into this component's props.
All mutations are delegated upward through callbacks.

nodes.title: useSidebarTree row model
nodes.summary: useSidebarTree turns the flat file list into the ordered rows the sidebar draws. It builds a nested folder tree, applies the active sort mode, and flattens it into a SidebarRow list of folder, file, and section rows. Folder expansion state is persisted per workspace so the tree shape is stable across sessions.
details: Row kinds are a discriminated union consumed by the renderer.
Sorting and flattening live here, not in the component JSX.

tour.title: Start at the store
tour.body: Workspace state lives in one reactive store. Everything the sidebar shows is derived from it, and persistence decides what survives a restart.

tour.title: How state changes
tour.body: User intent runs through the actions layer, which mutates the store and coordinates disk work. This is where new workspace behavior is wired in.

tour.title: Crossing into Electron
tour.body: Filesystem access only happens over the IPC bridge, gated by the grants system. Renderer code asks; main.ts decides and performs.

tour.title: Rendering the list
tour.body: The shared Sidebar is pure presentation: it takes files and callbacks and renders rows, leaving all state to the host app.

tour.title: Shaping the rows
tour.body: useSidebarTree converts the flat file list into the folder/file/section rows actually drawn, including sort order and expansion.

graphs.label: Data flow graph
graphs.summary: How pin state moves from disk into the store and back to disk as the user pins, unpins, renames, and deletes notes.

nodes.title: .hubble/config.json
nodes.summary: Versioned per-workspace config storing pinned note paths (relative to the workspace).
details: New file: { version: 1, pinnedNotes: string[] } written under the workspace's .hubble/ dir.
parseWorkspaceConfig drops the file on version mismatch and filters invalid/absolute/'..' entries.

files.note: workspaceConfig constants, parse/normalize, read & write IPC handlers

nodes.title: readWorkspaceConfig IPC
nodes.summary: On workspace open, the read handler parses config.json (or returns an empty config if the file is missing).
details: ENOENT is treated as an empty pin set, so a fresh workspace just has no pins.
loadPinnedNotes runs in parallel with refreshFiles via Promise.all.

nodes.title: workspaceStore.pinnedNotes
nodes.summary: Pins live in the store as absolute paths; the loader converts the stored relative paths to absolute on read.
details: absoluteWorkspacePath maps stored relative entries to absolute paths for the running session.
openWorkspace and persistence both initialize pinnedNotes to [] before the load resolves.

nodes.title: Pinned section rows
nodes.summary: The shared sidebar marks files as pinned and useSidebarTree hoists them into a top 'Pinned' section above the folder tree.
details: Desktop Sidebar builds a pinnedSet and sets file.pinned per row.
flattenRows emits a 'section' row plus pinned file rows, and skips pinned files inside folders.

nodes.title: writePinnedNotes IPC
nodes.summary: Toggling a pin updates the store, then writes the config back to disk via the write handler, which normalizes and grants the file.
details: writePinnedNotes converts absolute paths back to relative before persisting.
On write failure togglePinnedNote shows a toast and reloads pins from disk to stay consistent.

nodes.title: Rename / delete sync
nodes.summary: Rename, delete-file, and delete-folder remap or prune pinnedNotes in the store and rewrite the config so pins never dangle.
details: renameMarkdownFile rewrites the matching pinned path to the new path, then persists.
deleteMarkdownFile and deleteFolder filter affected pins before writing config back.

tour.title: Where pins live
tour.body: Pins are persisted in a versioned .hubble/config.json per workspace. Start here: this file is the durable source of truth.

tour.title: Loading on open
tour.body: Opening a workspace parses the config (empty if absent) in parallel with the file listing.

tour.title: Into the store
tour.body: Stored relative paths become absolute paths in workspaceStore.pinnedNotes for the live session.

tour.title: Out to the sidebar
tour.body: The store's pins drive a pinned flag, and useSidebarTree hoists pinned files into a top 'Pinned' section.

tour.title: Back to disk
tour.body: Toggling a pin updates the store and writes config.json back, with a toast + reload fallback on failure.

tour.title: Keeping pins honest
tour.body: Rename and delete operations remap or prune pins and rewrite the config so entries never point at missing files.

graphs.label: Code dependency graph
graphs.summary: Which changed modules depend on which. The WorkspaceConfig type is the shared contract; the IPC bridge and store actions build on it; the UI consumes store state.

nodes.title: WorkspaceConfig + DesktopApi types
nodes.summary: Adds the WorkspaceConfig type and read/writeWorkspaceConfig to the DesktopApi interface — the shared contract everything else is typed against.
details: Leaf dependency: defines the shape, depends on nothing else in this PR.
Both main and preload import this type.

nodes.title: electron/main.ts handlers
nodes.summary: Implements read/write IPC handlers, config parsing/normalization, validation, and path grants.
details: Imports WorkspaceConfig from the types module.
Owns the privileged disk side of the feature.

nodes.title: electron/preload.ts bridge
nodes.summary: Exposes readWorkspaceConfig / writeWorkspaceConfig on desktopApi, forwarding to the IPC channels.
details: Thin pass-through that fulfills the DesktopApi contract.
Renderer code calls these, never ipcRenderer directly.

nodes.title: store/actions.ts
nodes.summary: Top of the renderer-side logic: loads/writes pins through desktopApi and exposes togglePinnedNote, plus rename/delete upkeep.
details: Depends on desktopApi (preload contract) and the workspace store.
togglePinnedNote is the action the UI binds to.

nodes.title: store/persistence.ts
nodes.summary: Adds pinnedNotes to WorkspaceState and initializes it empty on hydrate.
details: Pins are recomputed from disk on open, not persisted to localStorage.
Shared state shape used by actions and the store.

nodes.title: desktop Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Binds store pinnedNotes to the shared Sidebar: computes pinnedSet, sets per-file pinned, wires onTogglePinnedFile to togglePinnedNote.
details: Bridges desktop store/actions to the platform-agnostic UI.
Depends on actions.ts and the workspace store.

nodes.title: ui Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the Pinned section header, inline unpin button, Pin/Unpin menu item, RTL front-truncation, and shared action-button styling.
details: Consumes the SidebarRow model and SidebarFile.pinned.
Receives onTogglePinnedFile as an optional callback.

nodes.title: useSidebarTree.ts
nodes.summary: Adds the 'section' row kind and flattenRows, which hoists pinned files into the top section and skips them inside folders.
details: Leaf of the render path: pure data transform consumed by the component.
Adds pinned?: boolean to SidebarFile.

nodes.title: store/actions.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest coverage for load (missing/relative), pin/unpin round-trip, and rename remapping of pinned paths.
details: Mocks read/writeWorkspaceConfig on the desktop API.
Validates relative<->absolute conversion and config writes.

tour.title: The shared contract
tour.body: Begin at the type seam: WorkspaceConfig and the new DesktopApi methods define what every other layer agrees on.

tour.title: Privileged implementation
tour.body: main.ts implements the contract on the Node side — parsing, validation, and the grant that authorizes writes.

tour.title: The bridge
tour.body: preload.ts exposes the two methods on desktopApi so the renderer can call them without touching ipcRenderer.

tour.title: Renderer entry point
tour.body: actions.ts is where the feature is orchestrated: load, toggle, and rename/delete upkeep all live here.

tour.title: State shape
tour.body: persistence.ts adds pinnedNotes to the workspace state and seeds it empty, since pins are reloaded from disk.

tour.title: Adapter to the UI
tour.body: The desktop Sidebar adapts store pins into props and wires the toggle callback to the action.

tour.title: Presentation
tour.body: The shared Sidebar renders the pinned section, unpin control, and menu item from those props.

tour.title: Row model leaf
tour.body: useSidebarTree is the leaf transform that actually produces the pinned section rows.

tour.title: Where it's verified
tour.body: actions.test.ts pins down load, toggle, and rename behavior against a mocked desktop API.

graphs.label: User action graph
graphs.summary: What the user does to pin a note and how that flows through to visible feedback and persistence.

nodes.title: Sidebar file row
nodes.summary: Each file row in the sidebar exposes an actions (…) menu on hover/focus.
details: The action button styling is now shared between the actions menu and the inline unpin control.
Row end padding was tightened so filenames truncate later.

nodes.title: Click Pin / Unpin
nodes.summary: The actions menu shows a Pin or Unpin item; pinned rows also get an inline unpin button beside the ellipsis.
details: FileActionsMenu renders Pin/Unpin based on file.pinned with a pin-line icon.
The inline unpin button stops propagation so it doesn't select the row.

nodes.title: togglePinnedNote
nodes.summary: The callback runs togglePinnedNote, which flips the pin in the store and persists the config.
details: Guards on workspace membership before toggling.
Adds or removes the path, then writes config.json.

nodes.title: Pinned section appears
nodes.summary: The note jumps to a top 'Pinned' section with an uppercase PINNED label, filled pin icon, and front-truncated path.
details: Long directory prefixes collapse via RTL text so the filename tail stays visible.
Extra bottom margin separates the pinned section from the folder tree.

nodes.title: Failure handling
nodes.summary: If persisting fails, a toast explains the error and pins reload from disk to match reality.
details: togglePinnedNote catches write errors, toasts, and calls loadPinnedNotes.
Keeps the visible pins consistent with what's actually on disk.

tour.title: Where the user starts
tour.body: A reviewer should begin at the file row, the surface the user interacts with, and note the tightened padding / shared action button styling.

tour.title: The action
tour.body: The user picks Pin/Unpin from the actions menu, or clicks the inline unpin button that pinned rows expose.

tour.title: What it triggers
tour.body: Both paths call togglePinnedNote, which flips the store pin and persists config.json.

tour.title: Visible feedback
tour.body: The note hoists into the top Pinned section with the PINNED label, filled icon, and front-truncated path.

tour.title: When it goes wrong
tour.body: On a write failure the user gets a toast and pins reload from disk, so the UI never drifts from the saved config.
